CJD is a highly rare and complex prion disease (not viral or bacterial), and currently, there are no approved or late-stage clinical trials for prion vaccines in humans. Vaccine development typically takes many years, and any breakthrough would require a lengthy clinical trial process that would be highly publicized. Due to the biological challenges of vaccinating against prion diseases and the extremely short timeline to both dates (less than a month), the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed and then updated on Wikipedia by these dates is astronomically low. I place the probabilities at the minimum limits for each date, adjusting very slightly for the further date to represent generic model uncertainty.
